# PunchNotes

> Structured analysis of stand-up comedy. PunchNotes breaks down Kill Tony sets into their component parts — bits, beats, and individual joke lines — annotated with joke type, setup/punchline/tag labels, and crowd response signals. Built for comedy nerds, researchers, and writers who want to understand how jokes work.

PunchNotes is a comedy analytics platform. It ingests and annotates stand-up comedy performances at the joke level. The current dataset covers Kill Tony, the long-running weekly stand-up competition hosted by Tony Hinchcliffe and recorded in Austin, TX.

Every set is broken down into bits (distinct joke topics), beats (individual joke attempts), and lines (setup, punchline, tag, or fluff). Each beat is labelled with a joke type (e.g. observational, self-deprecating, callback, absurdist) and metrics like punch density and tag density are computed per set.

PunchNotes is a fan-made analytics site, not affiliated with Kill Tony or Tony Hinchcliffe. The dataset is derived from publicly available YouTube recordings.

## Key Pages

- [Kill Tony Hub](https://www.punchnotes.app/killtony): Episode playlists, comedian cards, and featured jokes
- [Episodes](https://www.punchnotes.app/killtony/episodes): All Kill Tony episodes, browseable by number and date
- [Comedians](https://www.punchnotes.app/killtony/comedians): All comedians who have appeared on Kill Tony
- [Sets](https://www.punchnotes.app/killtony/sets): Browse all sets with comedian and episode context
- [Jokes](https://www.punchnotes.app/killtony/jokes): Browse and search individual joke beats across the entire archive
- [Joke Originality Checker](https://www.punchnotes.app/joke-originality-checker): Free tool that checks a joke's originality — paste any joke or premise and it finds the closest matching jokes in the database using semantic similarity, with line-by-line highlights
- [About](https://www.punchnotes.app/about): What PunchNotes is and why it exists

## Data Model

Episode → Sets (one per comedian per episode) → Bits (topic clusters) → Beats (individual jokes)

Each Beat has a premise (core idea), joke_type (mechanism classification), and lines labelled as setup / punchline / tag / fluff.

Metrics per set: punch_density (punchlines to total lines ratio), tag_density (tags to total lines ratio).

Slug formats: episodes use `episode-NNN` (e.g. `episode-601`), comedians use lowercase-hyphenated names (e.g. `john-smith`), sets combine both (e.g. `episode-601-john-smith-1`).
